_meta.description: Emissions section registered input nodes. Includes propane consumption, fuel emission factors, reporting year, nuclear waste factor, and reference model inputs for cross-model emissions comparison. Emission factors are C-classified and can differ between Target and Reference models.

instances.label: Total Propane Use
instances.note: User-entered actual propane consumption from utility bills.

instances.label: Gas Emission Factor
instances.note: CO2-equivalent emissions per cubic metre of natural gas.

instances.label: Propane Emission Factor
instances.note: CO2-equivalent emissions per kilogram of propane.

instances.label: Oil Emission Factor
instances.note: CO2-equivalent emissions per litre of heating oil.

instances.label: Wood Emission Factor
instances.note: CO2-equivalent emissions per cubic metre of wood fuel.

instances.label: Reporting Year
instances.note: Calendar year for the energy report. G-classified — shared across all models.

instances.label: Nuclear Waste Factor
instances.note: High-level nuclear waste generation factor per kWh of nuclear electricity. Consumed by emissions.nuclearWaste (l_34).

instances.label: Reference Operational Emissions
instances.note: Reference model's operational emissions in megatonnes. Injected via REF_OUTPUT_TO_TARGET_INPUT.

instances.label: Reference GHGI
instances.note: Reference model's annual greenhouse gas intensity. Injected via REF_OUTPUT_TO_TARGET_INPUT.

instances.label: Reference Total Embodied Emissions
instances.note: Reference model's total embodied carbon. Injected via REF_OUTPUT_TO_TARGET_INPUT.

instances.label: Reference User Modelled Embodied Carbon
instances.note: Reference model's user-modelled embodied carbon intensity. Non-zero default from standard reference value. Injected via REF_OUTPUT_TO_TARGET_INPUT.

signature.inputs: These ARE the inputs (registered via graph.registerInput in EmissionsNodes.js)
signature.output: Values stored in MultiModelState, consumed by emissions calculations, nuclear waste, and cross-model compliance comparisons

preconditions.description: Emission factors must be positive for meaningful emissions calculation

invariants.condition: Emission factor defaults match national average values
invariants.description: Gas=1921, Propane=2970, Oil=2753, Wood=150 are Canadian averages

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of corresponding fuel emissions (g_28, g_29, g_30, g_31)
behavior.note: C-classified, allows different factors for Target vs Reference

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of propane emissions and energy subtotals

behavior.condition: Reference emissions inputs populated via REF_OUTPUT_TO_TARGET_INPUT
behavior.output: Enables S05 emissions compliance comparison between Target and Reference
behavior.note: All reference.* inputs start at defaults until Reference model computes

edgeCases.condition: Nuclear waste factor default is very small (0.0096)
edgeCases.handling: Produces very small nuclear waste values, which is expected
edgeCases.rationale: Nuclear waste per kWh is inherently tiny

edgeCases.condition: Reference user modelled EC has non-zero default (345.82)
edgeCases.handling: Provides baseline comparison even before Reference model computes
edgeCases.rationale: Standard reference value for embodied carbon comparison
